translations: That you may sit firmly on their backs then remember
(the) favor (of) your Lord when you sit firmly on them
and say Glory be (to) the One Who (has) subjected to us
this and not we were of it capable
puzzle.hint: This is part of a well-known supplication recited when mounting a means of transport.
puzzle.arabic: لِتَسْتَوُۥا۟ عَلَىٰ ظُهُورِهِۦ ثُمَّ تَذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَةَ رَبِّكُمْ إِذَا ٱسْتَوَيْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ وَتَقُولُوا۟ سُبْحَـٰنَ ٱلَّذِى سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَـٰذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُۥ مُقْرِنِينَ
puzzle.verseEn: That you may settle yourselves upon their backs and then remember the favor of your Lord when you have settled upon them and say, "Exalted is He who has subjected this to us, and we could not have [otherwise] subdued it.
